item.title: Unhinged, Unrelated, Dumperoony: Continued 19
item.description:
Lmao rofl
Imagine getting high and that table stalking you around the house. Imagine it being able to climb stairs. And open doors with its opposable thumbs. Oh, it's got hands. Hidden away. Nimble, deadly, quick little hands. Sweet dreams, LNI.
Well, when I say close, I mean....
Nice.
(record scratch) that's me, the coconut. I bet you're wondering how fast I'm flying.
The universe is vast and wonderous indeed.
I don't go much for Musk but this was a good result in anyone's book.
item.dc:creator: jaddodd

item.title: Memes That Go *Toot*
item.dc:creator: MsSpicyBrain

item.title: Cake Day Cat Dump (mostly Void, with Cat Tax)
item.description:
Which is totally true. Just three nights ago, we had turned off the lights, and then heard a repetitive "clink" coming from somewhere else in the house. My husband said, "He's just playing with something." I listened and said No. Sure enough, our male cat was on the counter, rhythmically licking the pan we had cooked sausages in, and hadn't washed yet.
This has absolutely happened to me.
And a Cat Tax — Our void Rona (short for "Corona" as we got her in the pandemic). Our grey cat's name is "Covie", short for... you guessed it! LOL
He always has stuff to say ;-)
item.dc:creator: hotforbagels

item.title: Family was told he died in ICE custody. Then they learned he’s alive — in a hospital in Guatemala
item.description: https://www.mcall.com/2025/07/18/luis-leon-allentown-grandfather-ice-guatemala/ Relatives of 82-year-old Allentown resident Luis Leon are headed to a Guatemalan hospital Saturday in hopes of reuniting with the man they say disappeared without a trace into the American immigration system a month ago — and who, for a time, they thought was dead. The last time anyone in the family saw Leon was June 20, when he went with his wife to a Philadelphia immigration office to have his lost green card replaced. There, the family says, he was handcuffed by two officers, who led him away without explanation. His wife, who speaks little English, was left behind and kept in the building for 10 hours until she was released to her granddaughter, the family says. Repeated inquiries to immigration officials, prisons, hospitals and even a morgue yielded no information. Leon’s name was not in ICE’s online database of detainees. Finally, on Friday, a relative from Leon’s native Chile was told he had been taken first to a detention center in Minnesota and then to Guatemala. The hospital, citing privacy rules, would not verify his presence there when contacted by The Morning Call. It is unclear whether Leon ended up in that Central American country deliberately or by mistake. A Supreme Court ruling in June reopened the door to the Trump administration’s efforts to deport immigrants to countries that are not their home countries. Leon was granted political asylum in 1987 after surviving torture at the hands of Chilean dictator Augusto Pinochet’s regime, according to his granddaughter, Nataly, who asked that her surname not be used because she fears U.S. government retribution against her and her relatives. In Allentown, he lived a quiet life, raising four children and enjoying retirement after years working at a leather manufacturing plant. It all fell apart, Nataly said, when he lost the wallet holding his green card and made the fateful appointment to replace it at the U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services office on 41st Street in Philadelphia. Frustration at not knowing Leon’s whereabouts turned to grief July 9, when a caller informed Leon’s wife that he had died, Nataly said. A family friend shared the information at that night’s meeting of the Lehigh County Board of Commissioners, where a number of activists had come to urge commissioners to stem ICE activity at the county courthouse. An ICE official said Friday the agency is investigating the matter but would share no other information, refusing even to confirm that Leon was at the Philadelphia office in June. Nataly, for her part, has run the gamut from confusion to grief to frustrated rage — often in the course of a few hours — as she has tried to learn her grandfather’s fate. On Friday, after hearing he was in Guatemala, she tearfully said she wants the world to know how he’s been treated by the immigration system. “I can see all my family is in pain right now,” she said. The mystery surrounding Leon’s ordeal goes beyond ICE. Just days after his arrest, a woman claiming to be an immigration lawyer placed an unsolicited call to Leon’s wife and said she could help get Leon out on bail, but didn’t say where he was or how she learned about the case. It was this woman who called to tell his wife that Leon was dead. A week after communication from the purported lawyer ceased, the family finally received word that Leon had been in detention in Minnesota and then transferred to a hospital in Guatemala City. Nataly said she intended to fly to Guatemala on Saturday to see her grandfather, whose condition is unknown. He suffers from diabetes, a heart condition and high blood pressure, among other conditions, she said. Nataly said the man she calls abuelo — Spanish for grandfather — is a well-liked figure around his Allentown neighborhood. He gardens, goes fishing with a close friend and, because he is skilled with tools, functions as a handyman for neighbors who need minor repairs. The Trump administration’s aggressive deportation program was initially supposed to be directed at undocumented residents who have committed crimes. However, the Transactional Records Access Clearinghouse, which gathers data on federal immigration enforcement, says the vast majority of people in ICE detention as of July 13 — 40,643 out of 56,816, or 71.5% — have no criminal convictions. Many of those with convictions were for minor offenses, including traffic violations, the organization said. Leon, according to his family, never had so much as a parking ticket — a contention borne out by court records.

item.title: Kalispéra, Imgur
item.description:
So you should know something about Frank
It may change your entire opinion of him.
Yes Frank. Caution indeed.
This guy? He farts.
And don't let this innocent look fool you. They are DEADLY. Like, "OMG, get up and move", deadly.
Bonus video of Frank playing fetch. What have I done. A farting fetching kitten.
Bonus bonus of me checking to see if Frank was dead. Not really, but man, this cat sleeps hard.
Bonus bonus bonus beans. Kaló vrádi guys. Tomorrow is Sunday, the day we clean and stuff. Hope your Saturday was great and you got to enjoy some fresh air.
item.dc:creator: FrankTheKitten

item.title: Fck me
item.description: Text wall incoming, feel free to keep scrolling. My wife made a new friend from work about 3 months ago. 1 month ago they started talking g daily because they are both into genealogy. The chats became more frequent and more intense rapidly, her entire focus shifted to him. First thing in the morning, check phone, chat with him. Last thing, in bed before turning over to sleep, text him. Now, before we got together, I never had anyone in my life other than myself to think about. For 28 years, there was only me, no one else. With my autism, I was okay with that. We had met previously on WoW (World of Warcraft) and I instantly fell for her then. Her then boyfriend told her to cut contact with me, and she reluctantly did. She was the one. The one who got away. Years later, she reached out on Facebook, she was single again and we instantly hit off, started visiting each other every month. 6 months later I moved in with her, uprooted my life to move to another country. Within a year we had a beautiful son, a year later, married, been married 8 years this August. Now, on our anniversary weekend, she had made plans to go travel a few hours away to spend a whole day with this new friend. She made the plans having forgotten, I had too when I encouraged her, to be a supportive husband. (Edit* I encouraged her and pushed her to make the plans on this date, having myself forgotten. It is very important for me that she does not come across as heartless, she is the most loving person I have ever met) When we remembered what weekend it was, she made no attempts at, or showed no interest in rescheduling because of train tickets (totalling 50 pounds) She has begun neglecting me emotionally in favor of this new person. I have expressed concerns of this, and how if elt hurt about this a couple of times already, but it always ends eith her stone walling me and going to bed. Last night, we had a wine night (we try to do this once a week) and the conversation dived into that topic again. I showed her the signs of an emotional affair, and she got upset about being able yo tick every single box, except the secrecy. (She only talks about him now. Shares their inside jokes and shows me all the things he says that makes her happy and cheerful) I expressed that if the only thing that stops them from being in a relationship is a kiss, then it is an emotional affair. She has told me they are emotionally dependant on each other. She has told me she loves him more than me, but in a different way. I didn't sleep last night. She has just now put on make up, grabbed a 1000 piece puzzle that he loves (which I bought for him as a present) and gone to his place to talk with him about how to save her relationship. (She did not specify which relationship) I am spiralling. Have been for weeks. I have not been the most attentive husband, because if autism and my entire life before her being on my own. I should have been more available. I failed her. I fear she will return to tell me that the love she still feels for me, pales in comparison to what she feels for him. Or that she will return to tell me that they have cut ties (which I told her I did not want. I just wanted to feel like I was the most important person again) and resent me for the rest of her life. I fucked up. My feelings destroyed my marriage.. Sorry for the wall of text. Thank you for reading, if you did. **update** She called me, told.me she needed a walk. I pleaded that she had not ended her friendship, and she told she had not, they would be friends for ever. I burst in to tears of joy that I had, for once, not destroyed a friendship of my wife. She needed to have a walk to clear her head, still awaiting her return for more details. She said she loved me before we said bye on the phone. I cam not believe how absolutely fucking blessed I am to have such an amazing wife. She truly, genuinely, is the best, most kind and loving person in the entire world. *which is why I got so fucking shit scared of me pushing her away)
item.dc:creator: Justanotherimgurbrowser
